title: Dinosaur Lab
summary: Dinosaur icons, Ice Age mammals, and prehistoric favorites become reading, sorting, number, and Top Trumps challenges.

audience.readingLevel: short fossil facts with parent-auditable numbers

sources.label: Natural History Museum dinosaur guides
sources.note: General dinosaur size, diet, and discovery notes.

sources.label: Encyclopaedia Britannica dinosaurs
sources.note: General dinosaur background and fossil context.

sources.label: Wikimedia Commons life-restoration paleoart
sources.note: Local gameplay images use dinosaur life-restoration media from Commons file pages.

sources.label: DinosaurPictures.org dinosaur profiles
sources.note: Visual and kid-friendly dinosaur profile reference.

sources.label: Dinopedia dinosaur articles
sources.note: General dinosaur background and species context.

sources.note: General dinosaur facts and cross-checking context.

sources.label: Britannica woolly mammoth facts
sources.note: Woolly mammoth size, diet, adaptations, and extinction context.

cards.name: Tyrannosaurus rex
cards.fact: T. rex was a giant meat-eating theropod with banana-sized teeth and one of the strongest bites in dinosaur history.
readingPrompts: What body part made T. rex such a powerful predator?

cards.name: Triceratops
cards.fact: Triceratops had three horns and a huge bony frill, useful for defense, display, and pushing through tough plants.
readingPrompts: How many horns did Triceratops have?

cards.name: Stegosaurus
cards.fact: Stegosaurus carried tall plates along its back and sharp tail spikes that could make predators think twice.
readingPrompts: What weapon did Stegosaurus carry on its tail?

cards.name: Velociraptor
cards.fact: Velociraptor was a small feathered hunter, much closer to turkey-sized than the movie monster version.
readingPrompts: Was Velociraptor giant or small?

cards.name: Spinosaurus
cards.imageCredit: Artwork by Joschua Knüppe. Article by Jamale Ijouiher. / Wikimedia Commons
cards.fact: Spinosaurus had a tall sail on its back and a crocodile-like snout that points to a fish-heavy diet.
readingPrompts: What clue tells us Spinosaurus may have eaten fish?

cards.name: Brachiosaurus
cards.fact: Brachiosaurus had long front legs and a high neck, helping it browse leaves far above many other plant eaters.
readingPrompts: Why was Brachiosaurus good at reaching high leaves?

cards.name: Diplodocus
cards.fact: Diplodocus was one of the longest dinosaurs, with a whip-like tail and a long neck for sweeping through plants.
readingPrompts: Which body parts made Diplodocus look so long?

cards.name: Ankylosaurus
cards.fact: Ankylosaurus wore bony armor and carried a heavy tail club, making it a walking tank of the Cretaceous.
readingPrompts: What made Ankylosaurus so hard to attack?

cards.name: Allosaurus
cards.fact: Allosaurus was a large Jurassic predator with strong arms, sharp claws, and slicing teeth.
readingPrompts: Which tools helped Allosaurus hunt?

cards.name: Parasaurolophus
cards.fact: Parasaurolophus had a long hollow crest that may have helped it make deep calls across the herd.
readingPrompts: What may the crest have helped Parasaurolophus do?

cards.name: Iguanodon
cards.fact: Iguanodon had thumb spikes and could move on two legs or four, depending on what it was doing.
readingPrompts: What special weapon did Iguanodon have on its hand?

cards.name: Carnotaurus
cards.fact: Carnotaurus had short horns above its eyes and tiny arms, but it was still a fast meat-eating hunter.
readingPrompts: What feature made Carnotaurus look different from many predators?

cards.name: Deinonychus
cards.fact: Deinonychus had a large sickle-shaped claw on each foot, a clue that changed how scientists imagined active dinosaurs.
readingPrompts: What kind of claw made Deinonychus famous?

cards.name: Edmontosaurus
cards.fact: Edmontosaurus was a duck-billed herbivore that chewed tough plants with batteries of tightly packed teeth.
readingPrompts: What helped Edmontosaurus chew tough plants?

cards.name: Pachycephalosaurus
cards.fact: Pachycephalosaurus had a thick domed skull, one of the strangest head shapes in the dinosaur world.
readingPrompts: Which skull feature made Pachycephalosaurus stand out?

cards.name: Therizinosaurus
cards.fact: Therizinosaurus had enormous scythe-like claws, even though it was probably more of a plant eater than a hunter.
readingPrompts: Why is Therizinosaurus surprising?

cards.name: Archaeopteryx
cards.fact: Archaeopteryx had feathers, wings, teeth, and a long bony tail, making it a famous early bird-like dinosaur.
readingPrompts: What features made Archaeopteryx bird-like?

cards.name: Utahraptor
cards.fact: Utahraptor was a much larger raptor than Velociraptor, with powerful legs and a huge curved claw.
readingPrompts: How was Utahraptor different from Velociraptor?

cards.name: Maiasaura
cards.fact: Maiasaura means good mother lizard, a name inspired by nesting sites with eggs and young dinosaurs.
readingPrompts: What discovery helped inspire the name Maiasaura?

cards.name: Dilophosaurus
cards.imageCredit: Petr Menshikov ( https://twitter.com/Petr75113553 ; https://vk.com/prehistoricproduction ) / Wikimedia Commons
cards.fact: Dilophosaurus had two thin crests on its head; real fossils do not show the movie-style neck frill.
readingPrompts: What did Dilophosaurus really have on its head?

cards.name: Brontosaurus
cards.fact: Brontosaurus was the classic 'thunder lizard,' a long-necked plant eater that became one of the most famous sauropods ever.
readingPrompts: Why do people call Brontosaurus the thunder lizard?

cards.name: Giganotosaurus
cards.fact: Giganotosaurus was a huge meat-eating dinosaur from South America, close to T. rex size but from a different dinosaur family.
readingPrompts: Which continent did Giganotosaurus live on?

cards.name: Gallimimus
cards.fact: Gallimimus looked like a giant ostrich with a long tail, long legs, and a toothless beak built for speed.
readingPrompts: What modern bird does Gallimimus remind people of?

cards.name: Pterodactylus
cards.fact: Pterodactylus is the famous animal many people call a pterodactyl. It was a flying pterosaur, not a dinosaur.
readingPrompts: Was Pterodactylus a dinosaur or a flying pterosaur?

metadata.accuracyNote: Pterodactylus is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Mosasaurus
cards.fact: Mosasaurus was a giant sea reptile and a fan-favorite mosasaur. It lived with dinosaurs, but it was not a dinosaur.
readingPrompts: Was Mosasaurus a dinosaur or a sea reptile?

metadata.accuracyNote: Mosasaurus is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Pteranodon
cards.fact: Pteranodon was a big flying pterosaur with a long head crest. It lived with dinosaurs, but it was not a dinosaur.
readingPrompts: Was Pteranodon a dinosaur or a flying pterosaur?
cards.imageCredit: DataBase Center for Life Science (DBCLS) / Wikimedia Commons

metadata.accuracyNote: Pteranodon is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Quetzalcoatlus
cards.fact: Quetzalcoatlus was one of the largest flying animals ever, a giant pterosaur tall enough to look a person in the eye.
readingPrompts: What made Quetzalcoatlus famous?

metadata.accuracyNote: Quetzalcoatlus is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Plesiosaurus
cards.fact: Plesiosaurus was a long-necked sea reptile with four flippers. It swam in Jurassic seas, but it was not a dinosaur.
readingPrompts: What helped Plesiosaurus swim?

metadata.accuracyNote: Plesiosaurus is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Megalodon
cards.fact: Megalodon was a giant prehistoric shark known from huge fossil teeth, and it was one of the ocean's biggest predators.
readingPrompts: What fossil part is Megalodon famous for?

metadata.accuracyNote: Megalodon is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Woolly Mammoth
cards.fact: Woolly mammoths were shaggy Ice Age relatives of elephants, with huge curved tusks and thick coats for cold steppe tundra.
readingPrompts: What helped woolly mammoths stay warm in Ice Age habitats?

metadata.accuracyNote: Woolly Mammoth is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Saber-toothed Cat
cards.fact: Smilodon is the famous saber-toothed cat often nicknamed a saber-toothed tiger, with long upper canine teeth for tackling prey.
readingPrompts: Why do people call Smilodon a saber-toothed cat?

metadata.accuracyNote: Saber-toothed Cat is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Apatosaurus
cards.fact: Apatosaurus was a massive long-necked sauropod from Jurassic North America, with a whip-like tail and pillar-shaped legs.
readingPrompts: What body shape helped Apatosaurus reach high plants?

cards.name: Dimetrodon
cards.fact: Dimetrodon had a huge sail on its back and lived long before dinosaurs. It was a synapsid, closer to mammals than reptiles.
readingPrompts: What made Dimetrodon easy to recognize?

metadata.accuracyNote: Dimetrodon is included as a popular prehistoric animal, but it was not a dinosaur.

cards.name: Argentinosaurus
cards.fact: Argentinosaurus was one of the largest land animals ever discovered, a huge plant-eating titanosaur from South America.
readingPrompts: Why is Argentinosaurus famous among giant dinosaurs?

cards.name: Oviraptor
cards.fact: Oviraptor was a beaked, feathered dinosaur once blamed for stealing eggs, but later finds showed it may have guarded nests.
readingPrompts: What changed scientists' ideas about Oviraptor and eggs?

cards.name: Protoceratops
cards.fact: Protoceratops was a sheep-sized horned dinosaur with a neck frill, famous from fossils found in Mongolia.
readingPrompts: Which famous horned dinosaur group was Protoceratops related to?

cards.name: Baryonyx
cards.fact: Baryonyx had a crocodile-like snout and a huge thumb claw, and fossil evidence suggests it ate fish as well as meat.
readingPrompts: What kind of food did Baryonyx probably catch near water?

cards.name: Suchomimus
cards.fact: Suchomimus means crocodile mimic, a name inspired by its long narrow jaws and fish-catching spinosaurid body plan.
readingPrompts: Why does the name Suchomimus mean crocodile mimic?

cards.name: Ceratosaurus
cards.fact: Ceratosaurus was a Jurassic predator with a blade-like horn on its nose and bony armor bumps along its back.
readingPrompts: What feature on Ceratosaurus made it easy to recognize?

cards.name: Compsognathus
cards.fact: Compsognathus was a tiny, fast Jurassic meat-eater, once famous as one of the smallest known dinosaurs.
readingPrompts: Why was Compsognathus famous for its size?

cards.name: Corythosaurus
cards.fact: Corythosaurus was a duck-billed dinosaur with a tall helmet-shaped crest that may have helped it make sounds.
readingPrompts: What was special about Corythosaurus's head crest?

cards.name: Hadrosaurus
cards.fact: Hadrosaurus was an important duck-billed dinosaur because its discovery helped show that some dinosaurs stood on two legs.
readingPrompts: Why was Hadrosaurus important to dinosaur science?

cards.name: Kentrosaurus
cards.fact: Kentrosaurus was a smaller stegosaur with plates near its shoulders and long spikes along its hips and tail.
readingPrompts: How was Kentrosaurus protected?

cards.name: Lambeosaurus
cards.fact: Lambeosaurus was a duck-billed dinosaur with a dramatic hatchet-shaped crest rising from the top of its head.
readingPrompts: What head feature makes Lambeosaurus stand out?

cards.name: Acrocanthosaurus
cards.fact: Acrocanthosaurus was a large North American predator with tall spines along its back that formed a raised ridge.
readingPrompts: What did the tall spines on Acrocanthosaurus form?

cards.name: Albertosaurus
cards.fact: Albertosaurus was a tyrannosaur from Canada, smaller than T. rex but still a fast and dangerous late Cretaceous hunter.
readingPrompts: Which giant predator was Albertosaurus related to?

cards.name: Amargasaurus
cards.fact: Amargasaurus was a sauropod with two rows of tall neck spines, giving it one of the strangest silhouettes in the pack.
readingPrompts: What made Amargasaurus's neck look unusual?
